title: Best 6 5G Core Network Management Tools 2026: Network Operations Guide
description: Core network operations complex? Compare 6 best management tools for 2026. AI monitoring, auto operations & boost efficiency 40%!
content:
5G Core Network Management Tools: Complete Comparison and Review for 2026

5G core networks use cloud architecture and handle network elements like AMF, SMF, and UPF. Managing these distributed components across your network takes specialized tools. We're breaking down the top 5G core network management tools available today to help telecom operators pick the right one for their needs.

What You Need to Know About 5G Core Networks

Your 5G core network runs on several key elements: AMF handles access and mobility (registration, connections, handovers), SMF manages your sessions (setting up, changing, and ending PDU sessions), UPF processes user data traffic (routing, forwarding, charging), NRF keeps track of network functions, and PCF controls your policies. Each piece has to work together smoothly.

Moving to cloud-based 5G cores brings some real challenges. Your network elements are spread across different data centers - you need one system to manage them all. Container deployment means things change dynamically. Network slicing requires managing each slice separately. And automation needs AI-powered tools to work well. Good management software should handle all of this: manage distributed elements, adjust containers on the fly, track slices individually, and run automated operations.

What should your 5G core management tool actually do? Monitor network element status, configure parameters, manage sessions, handle slices, diagnose problems, and optimize performance. Real-time monitoring keeps you informed about what's running. Configuration management adjusts how things operate. Session management tracks PDU sessions. Slice management separates resources and operations for different slices.

Our Deep Dive into the Top Tools

1. Nokia 5G Core Manager

Nokia's Core Manager handles all their 5G core network elements. It tracks AMF, SMF, UPF, and other elements in real-time. You can flexibly adjust parameters, monitor PDU sessions, manage different slices, and automatically diagnose problems.

What makes Nokia CM stand out? It's tightly integrated with Nokia equipment - you get deep visibility into every element's internal status for precise monitoring. The slice management creates and adjusts slices dynamically. Automated operations recover from faults automatically. AI-driven performance optimization suggests improvements. Cloud deployment manages distributed elements from one place.

2. Ericsson 5G Core Operations

Ericsson's Core Operations manages their 5G core elements. You get comprehensive status monitoring, flexible parameter adjustment, PDU session tracking, slice-level management, and AI-powered automated operations.

Ericsson CO's strength is its AI intelligence. AI-driven monitoring predicts performance issues before they happen. Automated operations handle fault recovery and parameter adjustment automatically. Slice management orchestrates slices dynamically. Cloud management handles distributed elements. Deep integration with Ericsson 5G cores ensures accurate management.

3. Huawei 5G Core Operations

Huawei's Core Operations manages their core elements with comprehensive monitoring, flexible configuration, session tracking, and slice management. It's adapted for domestic operator requirements.

Huawei CO shines in localization support. It's deeply adapted to domestic operators' processes and regulatory requirements. Flexible customization lets operators adjust management functions. Strong technical support provides local services. Deep integration with Huawei cores ensures accuracy. Automated operations reduce manual work.

4. Oracle 5G Core Management

Oracle's Core Management provides universal core network management. It supports standard interfaces for monitoring, flexible configuration, session tracking, slice management, and distributed cloud deployment.

Oracle CM's advantage is its reliable tech stack. Oracle databases ensure data security and performance. Flexible management handles different vendors' elements. Cloud deployment cuts operations costs. Visualization dashboards show core network status. AI analysis provides performance insights.

What's Coming in 2026

5G core network management is heading toward more intelligence, automation, and cloud-based operations. AI will become central - automatically monitoring elements, predicting problems, optimizing configurations. Automated operations will cut manual work and boost efficiency. Cloud management will become the standard for handling distributed elements.

Slice management will be a priority. Tools need to create, adjust, and delete slices dynamically. Slice-level monitoring and fault location will get more detailed. Edge core network management will become necessary - tools must manage elements distributed at the edge.

2026 is when 5G core network management goes intelligent. Mature AI technology brings smarter monitoring and optimization. Automation reduces manual workload. Cloud management unifies distributed elements. Operators should pick core network management tools with AI analysis, automated operations, and slice management to prepare for 5G core operations.
author: Telecom Expert
